creator: nathan@rtfm.net
text:
When a filename has a space in it, xmpeg3 only recognizes
	the part of the filename to the right of the rightmost space--
	including when passing arguments to amp. Of course, any files
	with spaces will thus not play.

Fix: 

Ewww...tcl/tk. Someone else better do it :-)
How-To-Repeat: 
	Make a few mp3 files with spaces in their names (as mp3 files
	come, more often than not) and run xmpeg3.
